phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your wall-hop bleed bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — wall-hop bleed bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your wall-hop bleed bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — wall-hop bleed bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your wall-hop bleed bursts on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your wall-hop bleed bursts in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Anivia's wall-frost poke control own this window — your wall-hop bleed bursts don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: W the wave and his chest — the return pass is the damage; stand where the boomerang's geometry doubles.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q-leap trades begin: W-Q-auto procs the bleed at 2 and most mids don't budget for it.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: the parkour opens — every wall on mid's border is now a gank route or an exit visa.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Bleed-string the contacts: W both passes, Q the gap, auto the third stack. The lane bleeds rent.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Shadow Assault online — the roam game doubles and the all-in wraps targets in a blade carousel.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: the full string one-rotations squishies. Shove and parkour — side lanes owe you taxes.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Respect window: at two-plus items the stardust ramp control beat your wall-hop bleed bursts cleanly. Trade only off Aurelion Sol's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.why: Bleed-string the contacts: W both passes, Q the gap, auto the third stack. The lane bleeds rent.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — wall-hop bleed bursts edge out the windwall dash-weaving when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving edge out your wall-hop bleed bursts on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Talon flanks from coordinates wards don't cover: R in, string the carry, blade-exit through the wall.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: W the wave and his chest — the return pass is the damage; stand where the boomerang's geometry doubles.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q-leap trades begin: W-Q-auto procs the bleed at 2 and most mids don't budget for it.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: the parkour opens — every wall on mid's border is now a gank route or an exit visa.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Bleed-string the contacts: W both passes, Q the gap, auto the third stack. The lane bleeds rent.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Shadow Assault online — the roam game doubles and the all-in wraps targets in a blade carousel.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yone's double-blade spirit dives edge out your wall-hop bleed bursts on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.why: Late Talon flanks from coordinates wards don't cover: R in, string the carry, blade-exit through the wall.
